title: magnolia coriacea
intro: Magnolia coriacea, also known as the large-leaved magnolia, is a plant species native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its large, elliptical leaves and showy white flowers. The plant's bark and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Magnolia coriacea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and act as an antidote for poisonings. Herbal preparations of Magnolia coriacea often involve drying the plant's leaves and bark, then infusing them in hot water to create a tea or decoction that can be used to treat various ailments.
